title: VRP Signed Verified Stay Offer v0.1
description: Structural schema for a host-domain signed verified stay offer envelope. It validates payload shape; cryptographic JWS verification is covered by separate conformance vectors. 2026-06-22: reconciled with the reference-node runtime — the rich fields a real node emits (price breakdown, calendar freshness, host's-own alternatives, verification pointer, etc.) are now described here as OPTIONAL, so the schema matches the offer agents actually consume. No previously-required field changed; this is additive. The non-conformant runtime field `confidence` is intentionally NOT added (it is removed at the source — bookability is expressed by agent_permission.may_quote_as_official_direct_offer + availability; signature trust is the verifier's three-state result, not an offer-asserted value).

verification.description: Optional, envelope-level. Convenience pointer for an agent to resolve the host-domain JWKS and record when it verified. Not part of the signed offer.

host_alternatives.description: Optional, envelope-level. When the requested window is not bookable, the node MAY surface its OWN nearest open windows so an agent never dead-ends. Never a comparison to another property.

receipt.description: Optional, envelope-level VRP receipt: a verifiable booking record carrying independently signed attestations (offer + transport layers), each checkable against the host's own domain. Provenance only — never approval, a score, or a condition for the stay to be real.

canonical.description: Optional. true asserts this domain is the canonical source for the property.

id.description: Optional runtime alias of property_id; some node code keeps this for buildEnvelope() compatibility. property_id is canonical.

calendar_freshness.description: Optional. Freshness of the node's calendar imports at quote time, so an agent can judge how recently availability was synced.

capacity.description: Optional. Requested vs maximum guest capacity for the offer.

total.description: Optional alias of public_total (the website total). Agents SHOULD quote agent_total as the direct total.

no_add_on_fees.description: Optional. true asserts the quoted total has no add-on booking, service, or cleaning fees — the displayed price is the total price paid. The node's own fee structure, not an OTA comparison.

ota_comparison_total.description: Optional. Null unless a signed OTA price is present; agents must not synthesize an OTA comparison.

breakdown.description: Optional. Per-night price breakdown for the quoted stay.

adjustments.description: Optional. Neutral, signed line items that turn the rack breakdown into the quoted total (e.g. a week/two-week package rate). amount MAY be negative — a package rate lowers the rack subtotal. Never a discount/savings/OTA-comparison label.

reconciliation.description: Optional, machine-checkable: Σ breakdown.nightly_rate + Σ adjustments.amount === total. null when the offer is not priced (not bookable). Lets a verifier re-check the offer math.

booking.description: Direct booking facts for the offer. See spec/v0.1.md §5.1. The booking URL host MUST be on the offer's canonical_domain and the URL SHOULD encode the quoted stay via checkIn/checkOut/guests query parameters; those runtime rules are not expressible structurally here.

direct_booking_url.description: Absolute https URL the agent routes the user to. Its integrity derives from being a signed field inside the JWS offer (no separate URL signature). Actionable only while the offer's valid_until holds. See spec/v0.1.md §5.1.

offer_id.description: Optional. Stable id binding the quote to checkout (see checkout_binding).

checkout_binding.description: Optional. How checkout is bound to this offer, e.g. "offer_id".

payment_options.description: RESERVED / experimental — a name placeholder for a future OPTIONAL agent-native payment-rail binding (e.g. x402). Its shape, settlement, refund handling, and reconciliation are intentionally NOT defined in VRP v0.1 and MAY change. Fiat via direct_booking_url is the only defined payment path in v0.1: a node SHOULD omit this field and rely on direct_booking_url, and an agent MUST NOT treat its presence as a defined, honorable payment path. Two invariants will hold whenever the binding is defined: the payee is the host's own (never an intermediary, OTA, or HemmaBo), and rails MUST NOT gate discovery or verification. See spec/v0.1.md §5.2.

rules.description: Optional. Stay rules surfaced with the offer.

refund_schedule.description: Optional. The node's cancellation refund terms as a computable schedule, signed inside the offer. Each row: the guest receives refund_percent of the paid total when cancelling at least hours_before_checkin whole hours before the check-in moment. Evaluation is a pure predicate: sort rows by hours_before_checkin descending, compute floor((check-in moment − cancellation time) / 1 hour), and the FIRST row whose threshold is ≤ that value applies; no matching row (including cancellation after the check-in moment) means 0 %. Flooring makes fractional boundaries resolve in the node's favour and MUST NOT be re-derived with other rounding. An EMPTY array means no refund at any point; an ABSENT or null field means the node has not published computable terms (unknown — an agent MUST NOT invent a refund promise). Nodes MUST relay these rows verbatim to guests, never re-labelled into named tiers. See spec/v0.1.md §5.3.

hours_before_checkin.description: Whole hours before the check-in moment at which this row starts applying.

refund_percent.description: Refund percentage of the paid total when this row matches.

must_not_invent_discounts.description: Optional agent guardrail. true forbids an agent from inventing or applying discounts not present in the signed offer.

wording.description: Optional. Suggested wording an agent may use when citing the offer, e.g. "official host-domain verified offer".

source_authority.description: Optional. Host-verified direct-source identity, signed inside the offer so an agent can verify (not just read) that the node is the official direct source at 0% commission — a claim a reselling marketplace cannot sign.
